requirements.title: Zero Dynamic Allocation
requirements.description: The library shall not use malloc, calloc, realloc, or free.
requirements.rationale: Ensures deterministic behavior with no heap fragmentation or unpredictable timing, enables portability to bare-metal targets that lack a heap, and eliminates heap-related safety defects such as use-after-free, double-free, and memory leaks.

requirements.title: Caller-Owned Memory
requirements.description: All memory used by the library shall be caller-owned and injected via initialization functions.
requirements.rationale: Gives the integrator full control over memory placement (e.g., specific RAM sections), makes memory usage statically analyzable, and avoids hidden coupling between the library and any platform allocator.

requirements.title: Freestanding C11 Compatibility
requirements.description: The library shall compile under -std=c11 -nostdlib -ffreestanding without hosted C standard library dependencies.
requirements.rationale: Targets MCUs, DSPs, and safety-critical platforms that lack a hosted C runtime, while leveraging C11 features such as _Static_assert, _Alignof, and guaranteed fixed-width integer types from stdint.h.

requirements.title: Explicit Status Code Returns
requirements.description: All fallible functions shall return explicit status codes (JUNO_STATUS_T).
requirements.rationale: Provides predictable, deterministic error propagation without platform-dependent mechanisms such as errno, exceptions, or longjmp, which are unavailable or unsafe in freestanding environments.

requirements.title: No Silent Failures
requirements.description: The library shall not silently swallow errors or produce undefined behavior on error paths.
requirements.rationale: Aligns with the fundamental assumption that all code is fallible; every error must be surfaced so that integrators can observe, diagnose, and handle failures rather than encountering silent data corruption or undefined behavior.

requirements.title: Dependency Injection via Vtables
requirements.description: All modules shall use the vtable/module-root/derivation pattern for dependency injection.
requirements.rationale: Enables modularity by allowing implementations to be swapped at initialization time, and enables testability by allowing injection of test doubles through the same vtable interface.

requirements.title: No Global Dependencies
requirements.description: Dependencies shall be injected via initialization functions, never globally referenced.
requirements.rationale: Eliminates hidden coupling between modules, enables multiple independent instances of the same module type, and supports testability by making all dependencies explicit and injectable.

requirements.title: Precondition Verification
requirements.description: All public API functions shall verify their preconditions at entry.
requirements.rationale: Ensures runtime memory safety by failing fast on invalid inputs rather than allowing undefined behavior, which is critical in embedded systems where hardware watchdogs and debuggers may be the only diagnostic tools.

requirements.title: No Global Mutable State
requirements.description: The library shall contain no global mutable state.
requirements.rationale: Guarantees reentrancy, avoids hidden coupling between module instances, and enables multiple independent instances to coexist safely in the same address space.

requirements.title: API Vtable Validation
requirements.description: All modules shall verify that their API vtable and all function pointers within it reference valid (non-NULL) memory before performing any operation.
requirements.rationale: Ensures runtime memory safety by validating dispatch targets before invocation, preventing jumps through NULL or uninitialized function pointers that would cause undefined behavior on bare-metal targets.

requirements.title: Injected Dependency Validation
requirements.description: All modules shall verify that all injected dependency pointers are valid (non-NULL) before performing any operation.
requirements.rationale: Ensures runtime memory safety by validating injected dependencies before use, catching misconfigured or partially initialized module graphs before they produce undefined behavior.
